body.title: BUG (pre-existing): context-load run lacks violation_summary() its test expects
body.intention: I intend that the context-load violation-summary feature and its test agree — implemented or removed.
body.description:
**Problem (pre-existing — NOT introduced by the fs-store fix):** tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py expects `run.violation_summary(n, days)`, but HEAD's skills/context-load/run has no such function (4 tests red at HEAD, confirmed via git show + stash). The old SKILL.md Step 5.9 referenced `python run violation-summary` — a subcommand the run script never implemented (run takes no args). The context-load SKILL.md rewrite (T-context-load-fs-store) dropped Step 5.9 to match the executor. **Shape:** decide + do — either implement violation_summary in the run flow (restore session-start rule-drift surfacing; read the skill-violation telemetry log; wire into main(); re-add SKILL.md Step 5.9), OR delete the abandoned test. **Affected files:** skills/context-load/run, tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py, skills/context-load/SKILL.md. **Design rules:** none. **Scope boundary:** IN — reconcile feature vs test. OUT — other context-load work. **Test plan:** if implemented, the existing test passes; if removed, the test is gone and the suite is green. **Completion criteria:** tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py either passes or no longer exists; SKILL.md matches the executor.

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: DISPOSITIONED (decide+do): the violation_summary feature is NOT in the canonical context-load spec (CLAUDE.md /context-load table + SKILL.md = 10 steps, no violation step; feature dropped in the 10-step rewrite @52f9dd42). Chose DELETE over restore: removed tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py (tested a non-existent function); SKILL.md already matches the executor (no Step 5.9). Completion criteria met (test no longer exists; SKILL matches executor). Feature idea captured as follow-on T-restore-context-load-violation-rollup (Akien prioritizes). Folded into T-modernize-skill-writepath-tests-design-first cluster #2.
body.unproven_reason: Disposition is a decide-to-delete + fold-to-follow-on, not a red->green code change; proof_emitter proves impl-at-HEAD red->green, not a test-removal/routing decision. Lever needed: a disposition-proof for delete/won't-do/fold closes that verifies the artifact is gone and the intent is re-homed, rather than requiring a passing test that by definition no longer exists.
